title: Microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels
abstract_summary: The effect of Cr addition (0, 3, 6 wt.%) on near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steel strips is investigated. Cr increases δ-ferrite volume fraction and size while decreasing nano-scale κ-carbide precipitation, reducing as-cast yield strength but not ultimate tensile strength or total elongation. After 20% cold rolling, Cr-containing strips show higher strength due to enhanced slip band formation. A double hardening mechanism (slip-band and microband) is proposed, with κ-carbide controlling early-stage strain hardening rate.
research_objective: To determine the influence of Cr addition on the microstructures, mechanical properties, and deformation behavior of near-rapidly solidified austenite-based low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steels.
material_systems: Austenite-based duplex steel containing δ-ferrite and κ-carbide precipitates
main_process_variables: Chemical composition: Cr content of 0, 3, 6 wt.%
Near-rapid solidification: centrifugal casting in argon atmosphere, copper mold rotating at 600 rpm, cooling rate ~1–1×10^4 K/s
Stress relief annealing: 400 °C for 1 h before cold rolling
Cold rolling: reduction of 20% in thickness (multipass) to obtain CR20%
Post-rolling annealing: CR20% strips annealed at 600 °C for 3 h (CR20%+annealed)

sample_aliases.meaning: as-cast strips annealed at 600 °C for 3 h
as-cast strips cold rolled with 20% reduction after stress relief annealing at 400 °C for 1 h
CR20% strips further annealed at 600 °C for 3 h

key_sections.role: Background on low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steels, effect of Cr, and objectives of this study
Preparation of steel strips by centrifugal near-rapid solidification, heat treatment and cold rolling procedures
Microstructural characterization and mechanical properties of as-cast and cold-rolled strips with varying Cr content
Analysis of Cr effects on strength and strain hardening, including early/final stages and proposed double hardening mechanism
Summary of the main findings on Cr influence and deformation behavior
